recipes.name: Friday Fish Fry (Fried Flounder)
recipes.ingredients: Flounder fillets, buttermilk, cornmeal, flour, Old Bay seasoning, salt, pepper, oil for frying, lemon wedges, tartar sauce
recipes.instructions: Soak fillets in buttermilk 30 min. Mix cornmeal, flour, Old Bay. Dredge fish. Fry in 350°F oil 3-4 min per side until golden. Drain on paper towels.

recipes.name: Chicken Chili (Batch Soup)
recipes.ingredients: 2 lbs ground or shredded chicken, 2 cans white beans, 1 can green chiles, chicken broth, onion, garlic, cumin, chili powder, sour cream, shredded cheese
recipes.instructions: Sauté onion and garlic. Add chicken, cook through. Add beans, chiles, broth, and spices. Simmer 30 min. Serve with sour cream and cheese. Freezes well.

recipes.name: Lentil Soup
recipes.ingredients: 1.5 cups red lentils, 1 can diced tomatoes, onion, garlic, carrots, celery, cumin, turmeric, smoked paprika, lemon juice, chicken or vegetable broth
recipes.instructions: Sauté onion, garlic, carrots, celery. Add spices, cook 1 min. Add lentils, tomatoes, broth. Simmer 25-30 min until lentils dissolve. Finish with lemon juice.

recipes.name: Beef Stew (Batch)
recipes.ingredients: 2 lbs stew beef, potatoes, carrots, celery, onion, garlic, beef broth, tomato paste, Worcestershire, flour, rosemary, thyme
recipes.instructions: Coat beef in flour, brown in batches. Sauté vegetables. Add beef, broth, tomato paste, herbs. Simmer 2-3 hrs low and slow until beef is tender. Thickens as it cools.

recipes.name: Black-Eyed Pea Jambalaya
recipes.ingredients: 2 cans black-eyed peas, 1 lb andouille or chicken sausage, rice, onion, bell pepper, celery, garlic, diced tomatoes, chicken broth, Cajun seasoning, bay leaf
recipes.instructions: Brown sausage. Sauté the holy trinity (onion, bell pepper, celery). Add garlic, tomatoes, peas, broth, rice. Season generously. Simmer covered 20 min until rice is cooked.

recipes.name: Sardine Sandwiches (Friday Lunch)
recipes.ingredients: 1 can sardines in olive oil, mayo, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, celery, red onion, salt, pepper, bread or crackers
recipes.instructions: Drain sardines. Mix with mayo, mustard, lemon, celery, onion. Season. Serve on good bread or with crackers. Michael version: just butter and sardines, no mix-ins.

recipes.name: Sheet Pan Chicken Thighs with Vegetables
recipes.ingredients: 6-8 bone-in chicken thighs, potatoes or sweet potatoes, broccoli or green beans, olive oil, garlic powder, paprika, Italian seasoning, salt, pepper
recipes.instructions: Toss vegetables in oil and seasoning. Season chicken. Arrange on sheet pan, chicken skin-up. Roast at 425°F 35-40 min until chicken is golden and cooked through.

kid_steps.step: Wash the broccoli or green beans in the colander
Tear the broccoli into bite-sized florets with your hands
Toss the vegetables with olive oil in the big bowl until shiny
Sprinkle the seasoning over the vegetables and stir to coat
Pat the chicken thighs dry with paper towels and season both sides
Slide the sheet pan in and out of the 425°F oven with oven mitts

recipes.name: Grilled Cheese + Tomato Soup
instructions: Soup: melt butter, sauté onion 3-4 min.
Soup: add tomatoes + broth, simmer 15 min.
Soup: stir in cream + basil, heat through (don't boil).
Sandwiches: heat skillet over medium.
Sandwiches: butter one side of each bread slice.
Sandwiches: assemble cheese between two slices, butter sides out.
Sandwiches: cook 3-4 min per side until golden and melted.
Cut sandwiches diagonally. Serve hot with soup.
recipes.notes: Soup batch-cooks Saturday morning, reheats for lunch and dinner; freezes well. Pairing is Lent-safe and Friday-friendly.

kid_steps.step: Butter one side of each bread slice
Lay the cheese between two slices, butter facing out
Sprinkle the dried basil into the soup pot after the cream goes in
Open the tomato cans with a manual can opener (adult-supervised)
Stir the simmering soup so it doesn't catch on the bottom
Flip the sandwiches in the skillet (adult holds the pan handle)
Sauté the onion, run the soup from start to simmer, and cook the grilled cheeses through

recipes.name: Scrambled Eggs with Toast
recipes.ingredients: Eggs, butter, salt, pepper, milk (splash), bread for toast
recipes.instructions: Beat eggs with milk. Melt butter in pan over medium-low. Add eggs, stir constantly until just set. Remove from heat slightly early — carryover cooks them. Serve with buttered toast.

kid_steps.step: Crack the eggs into a bowl — adult helps with shell bits
Whisk the eggs with a splash of milk until pale yellow
Spread butter on the warm toast once it pops up
Stir the eggs in the pan while they cook (adult holds the pan handle)
Cook the eggs start-to-finish on the stovetop, melting the butter and pulling the pan early

recipes.name: Overnight Oats / Morning Oatmeal
recipes.ingredients: Rolled oats, milk or water, honey or maple syrup, cinnamon, fruit (banana, berries, apple)
recipes.instructions: Hot: simmer oats in milk/water 5 min. Overnight: combine oats and milk in jar, refrigerate. Top with fruit and honey. James version: soft, with mashed banana.

kid_steps.step: Pour the oats into the jar or pot
Pour the milk over the oats
Drizzle in the honey or maple syrup, then sprinkle cinnamon on top
Mash a banana with a fork for the youngest portion
Slice fresh fruit with a butter knife for the toppings
Simmer the oats on the stovetop for 5 minutes (hot version)

recipes.name: Roast Chicken Tacos (Leftover Night)
recipes.ingredients: Leftover roast chicken, corn or flour tortillas, shredded cheese, sour cream, salsa, lettuce or cabbage, lime, cilantro (optional)
recipes.instructions: Shred leftover chicken. Warm tortillas. Set up taco bar. Michael version: plain chicken in tortilla with cheese only.

kid_steps.step: Tear lettuce or shred cabbage into a bowl with your hands
Squeeze the lime wedges over the toppings bowls
Sprinkle shredded cheese into a serving bowl
Pull the leftover chicken apart with two forks
Warm the tortillas in a dry skillet and heat the pulled chicken in a pan with a splash of broth or salsa to keep it moist — lead the whole assembly.

recipes.name: Baked Salmon with Rice
recipes.ingredients: Salmon fillets, olive oil, garlic, lemon, dill or parsley, salt, pepper. Serve with white rice and steamed broccoli.
recipes.instructions: Season salmon with oil, garlic, lemon, dill. Bake at 400°F 12-15 min until flakes easily. Serve over rice with a vegetable.

recipes.name: Strawberry and Cream Cheese Finger Sandwiches
recipes.ingredients:
# Strawberry & Cream Cheese Finger Sandwiches

Light, sweet, and elegant — perfect for a high tea baby shower.

## Ingredients

- 12 slices soft white sandwich bread (crusts removed)
- 8 oz cream cheese, softened
- 2 tbsp powdered sugar
- 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
- 1 cup fresh strawberries, thinly sliced
- 12 fresh mint leaves (optional, for garnish)
recipes.instructions:
1. Beat 8 oz cream cheese, softened with 2 tbsp powdered sugar and 1/2 tsp vanilla extract until smooth and fluffy. Taste and adjust sweetness.
2. Spread the cream cheese mixture generously on all 12 slices soft white sandwich bread (crusts removed). Lay thinly sliced 1 cup fresh strawberries, thinly sliced in a single layer on half the slices, covering edge to edge.
3. Top each strawberry slice with a plain cream cheese slice, press gently, and wrap the whole tray tightly in plastic wrap. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes — this firms them up for clean cutting.
4. Using a sharp serrated knife, cut each sandwich into 3 fingers (or 4 triangles for a pretty look). Wipe the blade between cuts for clean edges. Garnish each with a small 12 fresh mint leaves (optional, for garnish) if using. Arrange on a tiered tray or platter.

## Notes

Make these the morning of the shower and keep refrigerated until serving. Don't assemble more than 4 hours ahead or the bread may get soggy. A thin layer of butter under the cream cheese acts as a moisture barrier if making further in advance.

recipes.name: Egg Salad Finger Sandwiches
recipes.ingredients:
8 large eggs
3 tbsp mayonnaise
1 tsp Dijon mustard
2 celery stalks, very finely minced
2 tbsp fresh chives, finely chopped
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp white pepper
12 slices soft white or wheat bread (crusts removed)
2 tbsp unsalted butter, softened
recipes.instructions:
Place 8 large eggs in a single layer in a saucepan, cover with cold water by 1 inch. 

Bring to a boil, then cover, remove from heat, and let sit 12 minutes. 

Transfer immediately to an ice bath for 10 minutes, then peel. 

Chop peeled eggs finely — not too chunky, not paste-like. You want a cohesive mixture that won't fall out of the sandwich. 

In a bowl, combine chopped eggs with 3 tbsp mayonnaise, 1 tsp Dijon mustard, 2 celery stalks, very finely minced, 2 tbsp fresh chives, finely chopped, 1/2 tsp salt, and 1/4 tsp white pepper. Stir gently to combine. Taste and adjust salt. 

Cover and refrigerate at least 30 minutes to let flavors meld. 

Spread a thin layer of 2 tbsp unsalted butter, softened on all 12 slices soft white or wheat bread (crusts removed). 

Spoon a generous layer of egg salad onto half the slices, spreading evenly to the edges. 

Top with remaining bread slices and press gently. 

Using a sharp serrated knife, cut each sandwich into 3 neat fingers. Wipe blade between cuts. 

Arrange on a platter, garnish with a sprinkle of extra chives or a small sprig of dill if desired.

recipes.name: Brined Roast Chicken
ingredients: 1 whole chicken (4-5 lbs) or split breasts
instructions: Combine salt, sugar, water, garlic, and thyme in large pot. Stir until dissolved. Cool completely (15 min in fridge or ice bath).
Place chicken in large bowl or resealable bag. Pour brine over, fully submerged. Cover or seal. Refrigerate 8 hours minimum (or up to 12 hrs). For split breasts: 6 hours minimum.
Remove chicken from brine. Pat completely dry inside and out with paper towels.
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Rub chicken lightly with olive oil. Season generously with salt and pepper inside and out.
Place chicken breast-side up on roasting pan.
Roast 40-50 minutes (whole bird) or 20-25 minutes (split breasts) until internal temp at thigh reaches 165°F.
Rest 10 minutes. Carve and serve.
recipes.notes: JP leads brine prep. Joseph assists with seasoning. Michael washes vegetables for roasting. Split breasts cook faster than whole birds.

recipes.name: Roasted Vegetables (Broccoli, Carrots, Snap Peas)
ingredients: 2 cups baby carrots (or carrots cut into batons)
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (same as chicken).
Toss broccoli, carrots, and snap peas with olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Spread on a sheet pan in a single layer.
Roast 20-25 minutes, stirring halfway through, until tender and lightly browned.
Serve alongside chicken.
recipes.notes: Can be roasted at same time as chicken. Joseph or Michael can prep vegetables.

recipes.name: Beef Pot Roast
ingredients: 1 lb baby potatoes, halved if large
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F.
Pat roast dry with paper towels. Season all sides with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear roast on all sides until deeply browned, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove to a plate.
Add onion and garlic to the pot. Sauté 2-3 minutes until softened.
Stir in tomato paste. Cook 1 minute.
Add beef broth, balsamic vinegar, thyme, and rosemary. Stir to combine.
Return roast to pot. Add carrots and potatoes around the roast.
Cover and transfer to oven. Braise 2 hr 30 min until roast is fork-tender and vegetables are soft.
Remove from oven. Let rest 10 minutes. Slice and serve with vegetables and broth.

kid_steps.step: Wash and chop carrots and potatoes into chunks
Rinse vegetables under cold water
Help sear the roast on the stovetop (with supervision)

recipes.notes: JP leads prep and searing at 4:00 PM. Joseph assists with potatoes. Michael washes vegetables. Roast goes into Dutch oven at 4:30 PM. Leftover roast + vegetables = Tuesday dinner and Wednesday lunch for John.

recipes.name: Baked Flounder with Rice and Roasted Vegetables
ingredients: 2 tsp fresh dill or 1 tsp dried dill
2 cups roasted vegetables (broccoli, carrots, or mixed)
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Pat flounder fillets dry. Arrange on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Drizzle with olive oil. Sprinkle minced garlic, lemon juice, dill, salt, and pepper over each fillet.
Bake 12-15 minutes until fish flakes easily with a fork and is opaque throughout.
Serve flounder over cooked rice with roasted vegetables alongside.

kid_steps.step: Pat fish fillets dry with paper towels
Prep vegetables for roasting
Arrange fillets on baking sheet

recipes.notes: Prep starts at 4:00 PM Wednesday. Fish cooks quickly—just 12-15 minutes. Joseph can prep the vegetables. Rice and vegetables can be cooked ahead and reheated.

recipes.name: Chicken Fried Rice
recipes.ingredients:
•	3 cups day-old cooked jasmine rice, cold
	•	¾ lb boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into ½-inch pieces
	•	3 eggs, beaten
	•	1 cup frozen peas and carrots
	•	4 green onions, thinly sliced (whites and greens separated)
	•	4 garlic cloves, minced
	•	3 tbsp soy sauce
	•	1 tbsp oyster sauce
	•	1 tsp sesame oil
	•	3 tbsp neutral oil (vegetable or avocado), divided
	•	¼ tsp white pepper
	•	½ tsp salt
recipes.instructions:
1.	Toss chicken with salt and white pepper. Mix soy sauce and oyster sauce in a small bowl — have it ready before you start. Fried rice moves fast.
	2.	Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wok or large skillet over HIGH heat until shimmering and just starting to smoke. Add chicken in a single layer. Don’t stir for 90 seconds — let it sear. Stir-fry until just cooked through, about 2 more minutes. Remove to a plate.
	3.	Add remaining 1 tbsp oil, still on high. Pour in eggs. Let set 15 seconds, then scramble into large soft curds — pull just before fully done. Push to the side.
	4.	Add rice, breaking up clumps before it hits the pan. Press against the hot surface and let sit undisturbed 1 minute. Stir-fry 2–3 more minutes.
	5.	Add frozen peas and carrots, garlic, and white parts of green onions. Stir-fry 2 minutes.
	6.	Return chicken and eggs to the pan. Pour sauce mixture around the edges of the pan. Toss together 1 minute.
	7.	Remove from heat. Drizzle sesame oil over top and toss once. Taste and adjust salt. Top with green onion greens. Serve immediately.
Notes
	•	Day-old cold rice is non-negotiable. Spread on a sheet pan and refrigerate uncovered if made same-day.
	•	Thighs stay juicy at high heat. If using leftover roast chicken, add it in step 5 instead of searing.
	•	Push your burner as high as it goes. The rice needs to fry, not steam.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

kid_steps.step: Wash and prepare vegetables
Beat eggs in a bowl
Stir-fry rice and vegetables over heat (with supervision)
Lead the entire stir-fry assembly

recipes.notes: Cook rice Sunday morning. Shred chicken Saturday night after dinner cools. Sunday lunch prep is just the stir-fry assembly (15 min active). JP can lead this solo. Double batch works well for leftovers.

recipes.name: Beef Pot Roast (Instant Pot)
recipes.cook_time: 1 hour 40 minutes (90 min pressure cook + 10 min vegetable cook + natural release)
ingredients: 1 lb baby potatoes, halved if large
instructions: Pat roast dry with paper towels.
Using a sharp knife, stab the roast 8-10 times all over to allow seasonings to penetrate during cooking.
Season roast generously with thyme, rosemary, paprika, salt, and pepper on all sides, working seasonings into the knife cuts.
Add 1 cup water to Instant Pot inner pot. Place trivet in pot.
Place seasoned roast on trivet.
Close lid. Set to Manual (or Pressure Cook) HIGH for 90 minutes.
Allow natural release when cooking completes (do not quick release). This takes 10-15 minutes.
Carefully remove roast. Check for doneness — meat should be tender and falling apart or nearly there. If not quite done, return to pot and cook 10 more minutes on manual.
Remove trivet. Place roast back in pot with liquid.
Add baby potatoes, carrots, and onion around roast.
Close lid. Set to Manual HIGH for 10 minutes.
Quick release pressure when done.
Remove roast and vegetables. Set aside on serving platter.

Make gravy: Whisk cornstarch and 2 tbsp water together to form slurry. Turn Instant Pot to sauté mode. Stir slurry into pot liquid. Simmer 2-3 minutes until thickened and no lumps remain. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve roast and vegetables over rice. Spoon gravy over top.

kid_steps.step: Stab the roast with a knife to allow seasonings to flow through — work carefully with adult supervision
Measure and pour seasonings onto the roast
Add water to the pot and place trivet
Help arrange vegetables around the roast after first cook

recipes.notes: Instant Pot version is faster and more hands-off than oven braising. Natural release is key for tender, juicy meat. Serve over rice instead of with potatoes as a side. JP can supervise the whole process; Joseph can help with vegetable prep and button-pushing; Michael can measure seasonings and pour water.

recipes.name: Classic Irish Oatmeal
recipes.ingredients:
2 tablespoons butter
1 cup steel cut oats
3 cups water
1/2 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
1/3 cup half and half
1/4 cup brown sugar
1 cup strawberries
6 ounces blackberries
6 ounces blueberries
3 tablespoons granulated sugar
1 tablespoon water
recipes.instructions: Press Sauté; melt butter in Instant Pot. Add oats; cook about 5 minutes, stirring frequently. Add water, salt and cinnamon; cook and stir 1 minute. Secure lid and move pressure release valve to Sealing position. Select Manual; cook at high pressure 13 minutes. Meanwhile, prepare Berry Compote, if desired. When cooking is complete, press Cancel to turn off heat. Use natural release for 10 minutes, then release remaining pressure. Stir oats until smooth. Add half-and-half and brown sugar; stir until well blended. If a thicker porridge is desired, press Sauté and cook 2 to 3 minutes or until desired thickness, stirring constantly. (Porridge will also thicken upon standing.) Serve with Berry Compote. Combine 1 strawberries, blackberries, blueberries, granulated sugar and 1 tablespoon water in medium saucepan; bring to a simmer over medium heat. Cook 8 to 9 minutes or until berries are tender but still hold their shape, stirring occasionally.

recipes.name: Bread Machine Pizza Dough
recipes.ingredients:
1 cup warm water (about 80 degrees F)
2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil
3 cups all-purpose flour
2 teaspoons sugar
1 1/2 teaspoons kosher salt
1 teaspoon active dry yeast
2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil
1 cup pizza sauce
1 pound part-skim low-moisture mozzarella, grated (about 4 cups)
recipes.instructions:
For the dough: Add the dough ingredients to the bread pan of a 2-pound capacity bread machine in the following order: water, olive oil, flour, sugar, salt and yeast. Program the machine for pizza dough (or just the dough setting, depending on your machine) and start the cycle (see Cook's Note).
For the pizza: Drizzle a rimmed baking sheet with the olive oil.
Once the dough is puffy and the dough cycle is complete, between 45 and 90 minutes depending on your bread machine, stretch the dough into the same shape and size as the baking sheet and press it into the pan—it's ok if the dough is a little small, it will become easier to stretch as it rests. Let the dough rest, covered, until it is at room temperature and relaxed, about 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F.
Dock the dough all over with a fork, then spread the pizza sauce evenly over the dough. Sprinkle with the mozzarella and any other toppings if using. Bake until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the sides and bottom crust are golden brown, 30 to 35 minutes. Let cool for 5 minutes, then cut and serve.

recipes.name: Frozen Corn (Heated)
instructions: Pour frozen corn into a pot
Add butter
Heat over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until corn is heated through (about 5 minutes)
Season with salt and pepper
Serve hot

kid_steps.step: Pour corn into pot
Stir corn while it heats

recipes.notes: Michael's specialty — simple, safe, and he loves stirring.

recipes.name: Caesar Salad
ingredients: 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese, grated or shaved
instructions: Open salad kit and pour lettuce into a large bowl
Drizzle Caesar dressing over lettuce
Toss until evenly coated
Top with Parmesan cheese
Serve immediately

kid_steps.step: Pour lettuce into the bowl
Toss the salad with dressing
Sprinkle Parmesan on top

recipes.notes: No chopping needed with the salad kit. Michael can lead this one.

recipes.name: Instant Mashed Potatoes
ingredients: Optional: milk or sour cream for creaminess
instructions: Bring water or broth to a boil in a pot
Add butter and stir until melted
Remove from heat
Add instant mashed potatoes and stir until smooth
Season with salt and pepper
Serve hot

kid_steps.step: Measure and pour instant potatoes into a bowl
Stir the potato mixture after the hot water is added (with adult supervision)

recipes.notes: Quick and reliable side. Joseph can manage the heating and stirring with light supervision.

recipes.name: Canned Green Beans (Heated)
instructions: Drain liquid from canned green beans
Place green beans in a pot
Add butter
Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until warmed through (about 5 minutes)
Season with salt, pepper, and optional garlic powder
Serve hot

kid_steps.step: Drain the green beans into a colander
Stir the green beans while they heat

recipes.notes: Simple, quick, and forgiving. Good for any helper.

recipes.name: Wild Rice
instructions: Rinse wild rice under cold water
Bring water or broth to a boil in a pot
Add salt and wild rice
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 40–45 minutes until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed
Fluff with a fork
Stir in butter
Serve hot

kid_steps.step: Rinse the wild rice in a colander
Measure water and rice carefully

recipes.notes: Start this early — it takes 45 minutes. JP can manage the stovetop work.

recipes.name: Sautéed Frozen Mixed Vegetables
instructions: Heat butter or oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat
Pour frozen mixed vegetables into the skillet
Stir occasionally until vegetables are heated through and slightly tender (about 8 minutes)
Season with salt, pepper, and optional garlic powder
Serve hot

kid_steps.step: Pour frozen vegetables into the skillet (with adult supervision)
Stir vegetables while they cook

recipes.notes: Joseph's role on Saturday — he can handle the skillet work with light supervision.

recipes.name: Spinach Pie (Frozen Crust)
ingredients: 1 frozen pie crust, thawed in fridge overnight
10 oz frozen chopped spinach, thawed and drained well
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F
Thaw spinach thoroughly and squeeze out excess moisture
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, feta, diced onion, salt, and pepper
Fold spinach into egg mixture
Pour into thawed pie crust
Bake 35 minutes until set and golden
Let cool 5 minutes before slicing

kid_steps.step: squeeze the spinach to remove water
whisk eggs and combine filling ingredients
pour filling into crust and slide into oven with mitts

recipes.notes: Pie crust must thaw overnight in fridge. JP can lead this meal.

recipes.name: Shredded Chicken Tacos
instructions: Warm shredded chicken in a pot over medium heat (3-5 min)
Warm tortillas in a dry skillet or over gas flame
Set out all toppings in bowls: cheese, tomato, sour cream, salsa
Let family assemble their own tacos
Serve with warm corn on the side

kid_steps.step: warm tortillas in the skillet
set out toppings in bowls
stir the corn in a pot

recipes.notes: Easy family assembly meal. JP leads warmup and coordination.

recipes.name: Ground Beef Pasta with Spaghetti Sauce
instructions: Boil water in a large pot for pasta
Brown ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it apart as it cooks (8-10 min)
Drain excess fat if needed
Add spaghetti sauce and canned diced tomatoes to beef
Simmer sauce while pasta cooks (8-10 min)
Cook pasta according to package directions, drain
Combine pasta and sauce in a large bowl or pot
Serve hot

kid_steps.step: boil water and add pasta
stir the sauce as it simmers
drain pasta carefully with adult help

recipes.notes: JP leads pasta; Joseph manages sauce. Thursday lifting day — good carb load.

recipes.name: Cajun Sausages with Wild Rice and Sautéed Vegetables
ingredients: 1 large bag frozen mixed vegetables (10-12 oz)
3 tbsp butter or olive oil for vegetables
instructions: Bring water or broth to a boil in a pot
Add wild rice and salt, reduce heat to medium-low
Cover and simmer 45 minutes until tender
While rice cooks, heat sausages in a skillet over medium heat, turning occasionally (12-15 min)
In another skillet, melt 3 tbsp butter over medium-high heat
Add frozen vegetables and sauté until heated through (8 min)
Season vegetables with salt and pepper
Serve sausages over wild rice with vegetables on the side

kid_steps.step: add wild rice to boiling broth
heat sausages in skillet, turning with tongs
sauté frozen vegetables in butter

recipes.notes: Saturday meal. JP leads sausage heating; Joseph sautés vegetables. Quick and satisfying.
